text: Whether evaluate code code block.  Use eval=FALSE show code reader without evaluated. Selective evaluation specifying numeric vector (R code blocks) also supported: vector must either positive (evaluate lines) negative (evaluate lines).  Lines excluded evaluation commented Stata -file.

title: Statamarkdown: Settings and functions to extend the knitr Stata engine — Statamarkdown-package
text: Whether show reader source code. Use echo=FALSE suppress source code document. specified numeric vector, indicates source lines show suppress.  example, echo=c(1,2) shows first two lines code block document (still evaluating entire code block).  Likewise, echo=-1 hides just first line code reader.

title: Locate the Stata executable — find_stata
text: Doug Hemken

title: Locate the Stata executable — find_stata
code: indoc <- ' # An R console example ## In a first code chunk, set up with ```{r} library(Statamarkdown) ```  ## Then mark Stata code chunks with ```{stata} sysuse auto, clear generate gpm = 1/mpg summarize price gpm ``` '  if (nzchar(Statamarkdown::find_stata()) &&     requireNamespace("rmarkdown", quietly = TRUE)) {   # To run this example, remove tempdir().   frmd <- file.path(tempdir(), "test.Rmd")   fhtml <- file.path(tempdir(), "test.html")    # Knit and render in a fresh R process, so that stale knitr state in a   # long-running session (e.g. from RStudio's "Run examples" button)   # cannot interfere with how the document text is parsed.   xfun::Rscript_call(     function(indoc, frmd, fhtml) {       writeLines(indoc, frmd)       rmarkdown::render(frmd, "html_document", fhtml)     },     args = list(indoc, frmd, fhtml)   )   message("HTML output created at: ", fhtml)   if (interactive()) {     # Show in the RStudio Viewer pane if available, otherwise the browser     viewer <- getOption("viewer", default = utils::browseURL)     viewer(fhtml)   } } #> No Stata executable found.

title: Extract Stata code from a dynamic document — purl_stata
text: input character string name input document. output character string name -file write.  Defaults name input document extension changed .. text character string document text use place file. documentation much documentation carry -file, following knitr::purl(): 0 (FALSE) extracts code ; 1 (TRUE, default) precedes code chunk Stata comment giving chunk's header (label options); 2 also includes document's text Stata comments (code non-Stata chunks included).

title: Extract Stata code from a dynamic document — purl_stata
text: -file written, path -file, invisibly. text given output NULL, character vector extracted lines.

title: Extract Stata code from a dynamic document — purl_stata
text: Chunks recognised knitr's chunk patterns, indented chunks fences three backticks handled.  chunk extracted header engine stata, uses older r chunk form engine = "stata" option.  Chunks purl = FALSE eval = FALSE options (either chunk header option comments) skipped.  Option comments forms knitr accepts Stata chunks (#|, Stata comment-prefix forms *| //|) recognised: never copied -file code, documentation >= 1 recorded plain Stata comments chunk header line.

title: Extract Stata code from a dynamic document — purl_stata
code: indoc <- ' Some text.  ```{r} library(Statamarkdown) ```  ```{stata first-Stata, collectcode=TRUE} sysuse auto, clear generate gpm = 1/mpg ```  ```{stata second-Stata} regress price gpm ``` ' purl_stata(text = indoc) #> [1] "* ---- stata first-Stata, collectcode=TRUE ----" #> [2] "sysuse auto, clear"                              #> [3] "generate gpm = 1/mpg"                            #> [4] ""                                                #> [5] "* ---- stata second-Stata ----"                  #> [6] "regress price gpm"

title: Convert a specially marked up Stata
Convert a specially marked up Stata
text: statafile character string name Stata "" file, containing markup comments. text character string place file. keep Whether save intermediate files. ... options passed knitr::spin

title: Convert a specially marked up Stata
text: path output file. given text instead file, returns compiled document character string.

title: Convert a specially marked up Stata
text: function takes Stata file containing special markup comments, converts knitr's "spin" format. turn sent knitr::spin, converted Markdown HTML (one several formats). Special Markup: "/*' " - Begin document text, ends "'*/" "/*+ " - Begin chunk header, ends "+*/" "/*R " - Begin chunk R code, ends "R*/" "/** " - Dropped document, ends "*/*"

title: Convert a specially marked up Stata
text: Doug Hemken

title: Convert a specially marked up Stata
code: indoc <- "/*' # Statamarkdown Example  This is a special Stata script which can be used to generate a report. You can write normal text in command-style comments.  First we load Statamarkdown. '*/    /*+  setup +*/   /*R library(Statamarkdown) R*/    /*' The report begins here. '*/    /*+  example1, engine='stata' +*/   sysuse auto /* Stata comment */   summarize  /*' You can use the ***usual*** Markdown to mark up text.'*/ " if (nzchar(Statamarkdown::find_stata()) &&     requireNamespace("markdown", quietly = TRUE)) {   # To run this example, remove tempdir().   fhtml <- file.path(tempdir(), "test.html")   # Spin in a fresh R process, so that stale knitr state in a   # long-running session (e.g. from RStudio's "Run examples" button)   # cannot interfere with how the document text is parsed.   x <- xfun::Rscript_call(     function(indoc) Statamarkdown::spinstata(text = indoc),     args = list(indoc)   )   writeLines(x, fhtml)   message("HTML output created at: ", fhtml)   if (interactive()) {     # Show in the RStudio Viewer pane if available, otherwise the browser     viewer <- getOption("viewer", default = utils::browseURL)     viewer(fhtml)   } } #> No Stata executable found.

title: Define a Stata engine for knitr — stata_engine

title: Define a Stata engine for knitr — stata_engine
text: Setting chunk option stata.fig=TRUE exports graph drawn chunk (Stata's current graph) figure file, includes output document.  figure laid knitr's usual plot machinery, standard figure chunk options apply, including fig.cap (figure caption), fig.alt (alternative text, accessibility; falling back fig.cap unset), .width, .height, fig.align, fig.link fig.path. export format controlled stata.fig.format chunk option, defaults "svg", Stata can export platforms batch mode (including console Stata Linux, export PNG).  PDF/LaTeX output set, example, stata.fig.format="pdf". hyphenated option spellings stata-fig stata-fig-format also accepted, matching Quarto's option naming convention (fig-cap fig-alt).  work YAML-style option comments (#| *| lines start chunk, either R Markdown Quarto documents), chunk header's comma-separated syntax, hyphenated name valid R. Note knitr's fig.width, fig.height dpi options control R's graphics devices effect Stata graphs; set graph size Stata, example xsize() ysize() options graph display.  One graph exported per chunk; include several graphs, draw separate chunks (using collectcode=TRUE carry data ).

title: Define a Stata engine for knitr — stata_engine
text: Doug Hemken

title: Define a Stata engine for knitr — stata_engine
code: indoc <- ' # An R console example ## In a first code chunk, set up with ```{r} library(Statamarkdown) ```  ## Then mark Stata code chunks with ```{stata} sysuse auto, clear generate gpm = 1/mpg summarize price gpm ``` '  if (nzchar(Statamarkdown::find_stata()) &&     requireNamespace("rmarkdown", quietly = TRUE)) {   # To run this example, remove tempdir().   frmd <- file.path(tempdir(), "test.Rmd")   fhtml <- file.path(tempdir(), "test.html")    # Knit and render in a fresh R process, so that stale knitr state in a   # long-running session (e.g. from RStudio's "Run examples" button)   # cannot interfere with how the document text is parsed.   xfun::Rscript_call(     function(indoc, frmd, fhtml) {       writeLines(indoc, frmd)       rmarkdown::render(frmd, "html_document", fhtml)     },     args = list(indoc, frmd, fhtml)   )   message("HTML output created at: ", fhtml)   if (interactive()) {     # Show in the RStudio Viewer pane if available, otherwise the browser     viewer <- getOption("viewer", default = utils::browseURL)     viewer(fhtml)   } } #> No Stata executable found.

title: Statamarkdown (development version)
text: Rendering several documents one R session now works: knitr restores chunk options knit hooks document, left second document without Stata executable path (failing “error running command”) without collectcode hook. engine now falls back executable located package attached, re-registers hook, longer necessary detach("package:Statamarkdown") documents. New chunk option stata.fig=TRUE: exports graph drawn Stata chunk includes output document. figure goes knitr’s standard plot machinery, fig.cap, fig.alt (alternative text, accessibility), .width, .height, fig.align, fig.link fig.path work R chunks. export format set stata.fig.format (default "svg", Stata batch mode supports platforms). Rmd Quarto documents hyphenated spellings stata-fig stata-fig-format also accepted using YAML style chunk options. (thanks @kylebutts #28) New function purl_stata(), Stata analogue knitr::purl(): extracts code Stata chunks R Markdown Quarto document -file, recording chunk’s header (label options) Stata comment, following knitr::purl()’s documentation levels (2 also carries document text Stata comments). Chunks purl=FALSE eval=FALSE skipped, including via option comments forms knitr accepts Stata chunks (#|, *|, //|). (thanks @davidaarmstrong #39) Fixed collectcode chunk option knitr evaluates chunks different directory document, example document subfolder RStudio project “Evaluate chunks directory: Project” option set, knitting rmarkdown::render(knit_root_dir=). temporary profile.now created, pre-existing profile.snapshot restored, directory chunks (therefore Stata) run. (thanks @DaniMori #17) Switch including html docs files including within package precomputed vignettes using Quarto. Tweak helpfile examples run within RStudio. Add markdown Suggests dependencies needed code path within spinstata (fact one helpfile). Guard two helpfile examples presence rmarkdown package. activate nocommands, nooutput quietly set TRUE Add tests Convert documentation use roxygen2

title: Statamarkdown 0.9.7
text: CRAN release: 2026-07-19 Fixed loss chunk output Stata log contain “end -file” line. spinstata() longer overwrites input file; statafile without .extension now error, extension check case-insensitive. Fixed crash collectcode chunk option engine.path given plain string rather list. cleanup temporary profile.created collectcode now locates knitr::knit() call call stack instead assuming hardcoded stack depth, making robust changes knitr’s internals, child documents, different ways invoking knitr. cleanlog=TRUE now removes lines multi-line loop commands wrapped (continued) command lines output. cleanlog=TRUE longer deletes genuine output lines begin decimal point. Fixed spinstata() block tracking marked-block starts ends line. Fixed error spinstata() R >= 4.3 producing Rnw Rtex output. find_stata() Windows now stops searching soon executable found, non-existent “StataNow19” directory stub removed search. Removed duplicated “Stata executable found” startup message attaching package without Stata installed. Minor performance improvements: Stata executable located package load, per-chunk processing avoids repeated system calls redundant vector allocations. Change maintainer Tom Palmer. Many thanks Doug Hemken creating maintaining amazing package. Add precomputed vignettes distributed CRAN package

title: Statamarkdown 0.9.6
text: CRAN release: 2025-10-07 find_stata() now searches /Applications/StataNow macOS, StataNow directories Windows, two additional installation directories Linux. Fixed bugs Windows directory search. Fixed help file cross-reference links new CRAN standard.

title: Statamarkdown 0.9.4
text: CRAN release: 2025-08-23 Updated find_stata() Stata 19 SSCC installation path.

title: Statamarkdown 0.9.3
text: GitHub-release, published CRAN. Updated find_stata() Stata 19.

title: Statamarkdown 0.9.2
text: CRAN release: 2023-12-04 Cleaner Stata log output, log cleanup now accounts unset chunk options. Fixed collectcode chunk option knitr >= 1.45. Package setup switched .onAttach() .onLoad(). Cleaned package dependencies CRAN submission issues.

title: Statamarkdown 0.8.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-06-01 Thoroughly reworked engine output processing package loading. Edited vignettes.

title: Statamarkdown 0.7.4
text: CRAN release: 2023-05-25 Fixed handling Stata executable paths containing spaces macOS Unix. Updated required knitr version.

title: Statamarkdown 0.7.3
text: GitHub-release, published CRAN. Updated find_stata() Stata 18. Improved help file examples, particular spinstata(). Changed vignette rendering, pointed README CRAN.

title: Statamarkdown 0.7.2
text: CRAN release: 2023-02-15 Better removal “Running …/profile.” line log long installation paths. Added Unix installation paths find_stata(), suggested Tom Palmer.

title: Statamarkdown 0.7.1
text: GitHub-release, published CRAN. Improved find_stata() directory search Linux, using Sys.().

title: Statamarkdown 0.7.0
text: GitHub-release, published CRAN. Updated find_stata() Stata 17, macOS fixes. Fixed problem factor variable names.

title: Statamarkdown 0.6.0
text: GitHub-release, published CRAN. Cleaned messages “notebook” mode. Fixed macOS path problem, added StataIC macOS search. Fixed log cleanup “Running …” line Stata 16, eval chunk option. Prebuilt vignettes binary version package. Added Philipp Lepert contributor.

title: Statamarkdown 0.4.4
text: GitHub-release, published CRAN. First tagged release: knitr language engine Stata cleaned log output, collectcode chunk option linking code blocks, spinstata() spinning specially marked-Stata -files reports, find_stata() locate Stata executable Windows, macOS, Linux.
